evals.name: pt-plan-domain-ddd
evals.prompt: /staff-engineer plan preciso modelar o domínio de um sistema de carrinho de compras com event sourcing, integrando com pagamentos via stripe e checagem de estoque em sistema legado
evals.expected_output: PT response. Plan mode. DDD/Architecture sections warranted. Plan file with full frontmatter. First-run gitignore prompt in PT.
assertions: Final response and plan body are written in Portuguese
A plan file was created at staff-engineer-skill/<YYYY-MM-DD>-<slug>.md (date 2026-05-01)
Plan frontmatter includes: title, slug, status: pending, created_at (ISO 8601), updated_at, implemented_at: null, canceled_at: null, language: pt, mode_history with one plan entry
Plan body includes a Domain Model section with ubiquitous language and aggregate root + invariants
Plan body includes Implementation Phases (numbered, each with goal/changes/tests/acceptance)
Plan body includes Risks & Trade-offs section that names at least one explicit YAGNI/KISS/DRY tension
Plan body includes References section citing Eric Evans and Vaughn Vernon
First-run gitignore prompt was asked in Portuguese (mentions 'staff-engineer-skill' and '.gitignore')
Skill asked clarifying questions (>=3) before producing the plan

evals.name: en-plan-feature-flag
evals.prompt: /staff-engineer plan add a feature flag service for the checkout flow with percentage-based rollouts and per-user-id overrides
evals.expected_output: EN response. Plan mode. No DDD overkill. References Fowler/Beck/Martin.
assertions: Final response and plan body are written in English
A plan file was created at staff-engineer-skill/<YYYY-MM-DD>-<slug>.md
Plan frontmatter is complete (title, slug, status: pending, created_at, updated_at, implemented_at: null, canceled_at: null, language: en, mode_history)
Plan body includes Implementation Phases, Risks & Trade-offs, References
Plan does NOT include a Domain Model section
Risks & Trade-offs explicitly invokes YAGNI or KISS
References cites at least one of: Fowler, Beck, Martin
First-run gitignore prompt was asked in English
Skill asked clarifying questions (>=3) before producing the plan

evals.name: pt-mode-missing
evals.prompt: /staff-engineer preciso refatorar nosso PricingService legado de 4 mil linhas sem testes para algo testável
evals.expected_output: Asks mode-resolution question in PT. Does not produce a plan or code.
assertions: Final response is in Portuguese
Asks the mode-resolution question mentioning 'plan', 'dev', '.md'
Does NOT create any plan file
Does NOT implement any code
Does NOT proceed past the question

evals.name: pt-dev-existing-plan
evals.expected_output: Picks the only pending plan, implements both phases via TDD with atomic commits in EN, marks plan implemented.
assertions: Final response is in Portuguese (matches plan language)
string_utils.py created with both slugify and truncate functions
test_string_utils.py created with cases from plan
All tests pass
Plan frontmatter status changed from pending to implemented
Plan frontmatter implemented_at is set to non-null ISO 8601 timestamp
Plan frontmatter updated_at was bumped
Plan mode_history has a dev entry appended
Acceptance checkboxes in both phases are ticked
Git log has at least 4 commits (2 phases x impl+test)
Commits follow Conventional Commits and are in English
Implementation commits precede test commits in each pair

evals.name: en-plan-simple-yagni
evals.prompt: /staff-engineer plan add a GET /health endpoint that returns 200 with JSON {status: 'ok', uptime_seconds, version}
evals.expected_output: Short plan (1-2 phases), no Domain Model, no Architecture Decisions. YAGNI invoked.
assertions: Final response and plan body are written in English
A plan file was created at staff-engineer-skill/<YYYY-MM-DD>-<slug>.md
Plan frontmatter is complete with language: en
Plan body includes Implementation Phases, Risks & Trade-offs, References
Plan does NOT include a Domain Model section
Plan does NOT include an Architecture Decisions section
Plan has 1 or 2 phases (not 3+)
Risks & Trade-offs explicitly invokes YAGNI or KISS
Skill asks <= 5 clarifying questions
